tests.description: A user can upload a JSON file to create a new data dictionary that is saved and visible in settings.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: A JSON file can be successfully imported as a named dictionary

test_coverage.verification_step: Dictionary is saved and shown in settings
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The newly uploaded dictionary appears in the user's settings after import

tests.description: A user can upload an Excel (.xlsx) file to create a new data dictionary that is saved and visible in settings.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: An Excel file can be successfully imported as a named dictionary

test_coverage.verification_step: Dictionary is saved and shown in settings
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The newly uploaded dictionary appears in the user's settings after import

tests.test_name: Create dictionary manually without importing a file
tests.description: A user can manually create a data dictionary by entering a name and defining terms directly, without uploading a file.

test_coverage.verification_step: Create dictionary with a single term manually
test_coverage.what_it_checks: A user can define a dictionary name and a term-definition pair without any file upload

test_coverage.verification_step: Dictionary is saved and shown in settings
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The manually created dictionary appears in the user's settings

tests.description: A user can rename an existing saved dictionary and see the updated name reflected in settings.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: A saved dictionary's name can be changed
The new dictionary name is displayed in settings after the rename

tests.description: A user can update the terms and definitions within an existing saved dictionary.

test_coverage.verification_step: Edit entries in an existing dictionary
test_coverage.what_it_checks: A saved dictionary's term and definition can be modified

test_coverage.verification_step: Updated dictionary is saved and shown in settings
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The edited dictionary content is persisted and visible in settings

tests.description: A user can designate a saved dictionary as the default dictionary.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: A saved dictionary can be set as the default
The dictionary correctly reflects its default status in settings

tests.test_name: Changing the default dictionary clears the previous default
tests.description: When a user sets a new default dictionary, the previously defaulted dictionary is automatically unset.

test_coverage.verification_step: Set a second dictionary as default
test_coverage.what_it_checks: A new dictionary can be promoted to default

test_coverage.verification_step: New dictionary is marked as default
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The newly selected dictionary shows the default status

test_coverage.verification_step: Old dictionary is no longer marked as default
test_coverage.what_it_checks: Only one dictionary can hold the default status at a time

tests.test_name: Upload an unsupported file format shows an import error
tests.description: When a user uploads a file in an unsupported format, they receive a clear error message.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: The system rejects files that are not in a supported format
The user sees a descriptive error explaining the file type is not accepted

tests.test_name: Upload a malformed JSON file shows an import error
tests.description: When a user uploads a JSON file with invalid formatting, they receive a clear error message.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: The system rejects JSON files that are not properly formatted
The user sees a descriptive error indicating the JSON content is invalid

tests.test_name: Upload a JSON with too many entries shows an import error
tests.description: When a user uploads a JSON dictionary that exceeds the maximum allowed number of entries, they receive a clear error message.

test_coverage.verification_step: Upload a JSON dictionary with too many entries
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The system enforces an upper limit of 500 entries per dictionary

test_coverage.verification_step: Error message contains 'may not exceed 500 entries'
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The user sees a descriptive error explaining the entry limit has been exceeded

tests.test_name: Upload an oversized XLSX file shows an import error
tests.description: When a user uploads an Excel dictionary file that is too large, they receive a clear error message.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: The system enforces a maximum file size for Excel uploads
The user sees a descriptive error explaining the file exceeds the size limit

tests.test_name: Create dictionary manually with multiple terms
tests.description: A user can manually create a data dictionary containing multiple terms and definitions.

test_coverage.verification_step: Create a dictionary with multiple terms manually
test_coverage.what_it_checks: A user can add more than one term-definition pair when creating a dictionary by hand

test_coverage.verification_step: Dictionary is saved and shown in settings
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The multi-term dictionary is persisted and visible in settings

tests.test_name: Duplicate dictionary name shows an error
tests.description: When a user attempts to create a second dictionary with the same name as an existing one, they receive an error preventing duplicates.

test_coverage.verification_step: Upload two dictionaries with the same name
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The system prevents two dictionaries from sharing the same name

test_coverage.verification_step: Duplicate dictionary name error is shown
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The user sees a clear error message when a duplicate name conflict occurs

tests.description: A user can export a saved dictionary as a JSON file download.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: The export action triggers a file download
The exported file is in JSON format

tests.description: A user can export a saved dictionary as an Excel file download.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: The export action triggers a file download
The exported file is in Excel format

tests.description: A user can permanently delete a saved dictionary, removing it from their settings.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: A saved dictionary can be removed by the user
The deleted dictionary no longer appears in the user's settings

tests.description: A user can supply a dictionary file during an analysis session to use it for that analysis only, without changing their saved default.

test_coverage.verification_step: Supply a dictionary file in the analysis panel
test_coverage.what_it_checks: A dictionary can be provided at the point of analysis rather than set up in advance

test_coverage.verification_step: That dictionary is used for the current analysis only
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The session-level dictionary applies only to the current analysis and does not affect saved settings
